compiled_state.title: archived is an inescapable status; the engine's own recovery hint is a command that always fails
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
archived is an inescapable status: reopen/transition/claim all fail, and the engine's own recovery hint is a command its own validator rejects.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. rebar archive <id>
2. rebar reopen <id> -> exit 10, hint: "Re-run: ticket transition <id> archived open"
3. rebar transition <id> archived open -> exit 1: invalid current_status 'archived'. Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked
4. rebar transition <id> open (auto-detect) -> exit 1 (auto-detect feeds 'archived' into the same validator)
5. rebar claim <id> -> exit 10. grep -rni unarchive src/ -> nothing.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: a working unarchive path, or at minimum a recovery hint that succeeds when executed verbatim.
- Actual: no CLI path out of archived; the suggested command always fails.

## Source
- src/rebar/_engine/ticket_txn.py:76 — hint built from actual_status verbatim even when it is not a legal current_status.
- src/rebar/_engine/ticket-transition.sh:121-146 — _validate_status whitelist open|in_progress|closed|blocked.

## Fix direction
Add an unarchive path (and/or accept archived as current_status for archived->open). NOTE: archive mechanics involve a marker (.archived) in addition to status — the validated mechanism details (event vs marker, list reappearance, claimability) are in the implementation-notes comment; follow it.

## Suggested RED test
archive -> reopen (or the emitted hint, executed verbatim) succeeds; ticket reappears in default list and is claimable. Hint text always names an executable command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A documented CLI path returns an archived ticket to open (RED test first)
- [ ] After unarchive: visible in default list, claimable, fsck clean
- [ ] ticket_txn hint never suggests a command the validator rejects
- [ ] transition/archive suites green

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (fix mechanics experimentally validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09) — MECHANISM CORRECTION, READ FIRST:
Archive = ARCHIVED event (reducer process_archived -> status="archived", archived=True) PLUS a .archived marker file (fast-path list exclusion at ticket_reducer/_api.py:281). The DESIGNED un-archive seam already exists: rebar revert <id> <archived-event-uuid> — ticket-revert.sh:180-196 removes the marker and _api.py:_is_net_archived computes ARCHIVED minus REVERT. BUT process_revert is broken: it only appends a revert record; replay still applies ARCHIVED, so state stays archived and list stays empty even after revert (verified). A bare STATUS archived->open event does NOT work either (_is_net_archived still drops the ticket at the list fast-path).
Validated two-part fix:
1) ticket_reducer/_processors.py process_revert, after the append:
   if data.get("target_event_type") == "ARCHIVED" and state.get("archived"):
       state["archived"] = False
       if state.get("status") == "archived": state["status"] = "open"
   (process_archived preserves deleted status; restoring open ONLY when status is exactly archived keeps delete semantics intact.)
2) ticket-transition.sh, immediately BEFORE _validate_status "current_status" (~:138): if current_status == archived, require target == open, locate the latest non-reverted ARCHIVED event uuid (scan event files: ARCHIVED uuids minus REVERT target_event_uuids, take max-ts), and exec ticket-revert.sh <id> <uuid> --reason="un-archive via transition archived open". ticket_txn.py is never reached on this path, so its :76 hint problem disappears for this case (still reword the hint defensively).
Validated: archive -> transition archived open (exit 0) -> ticket in default list -> claimable. Regressions checked: happy-path transitions OK; transition closed archived still rejected; archived->closed rejected with clear message.
CAVEAT discovered: .cache.json has NO reducer-version key (only dir-hash of names+sizes), so a ticket cached under the old reducer stays hidden until cache invalidation — add a cache version field with this change (relates to bug fond-ulna-ball / 1d76).
NEW BUG discovered and filed separately: process_revert never undoes the ARCHIVED projection (see linked discovered_from ticket).
Regression suites: test-ticket-transition*.sh (4 files), test-ticket-archive*.sh (4 files), test-ticket-archived-exclusion-e2e.sh, tests/scripts/test_ticket_reducer*.py (esp. test_ticket_reducer_marker.py).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
Joe Oakhart

verify_commands.dd_text: archived->open works end to end; ticket relisted + claimable
